output: Implemented the approved plan and captured execution evidence.
deviations: The repository's raw `npx vitest run ...` invocations from the plan did not load the configured `@` alias resolution, so `T1`, `T3`, `T6`, and `T7` were executed with `--config config/testing/vitest.config.ts`. The unconfigured baseline command failed before running tests with `Cannot find package '@/shared/contexts/usePaneLockPolicyState'`.

task_updates.executor_notes: Ran the planned baseline command first and hit the repo config issue, then reran `src/shared/contexts/__tests__/PanesContext.test.tsx` with the project vitest config. The configured run produced exactly 3 failures in the allocator tests: shrink-to-fit returned editor `420` instead of `300`, keeps-generations returned editor `420` instead of `200`, and degraded-proportional returned editor `200` instead of `156`.

Replaced `getEffectivePaneHeights` in `src/shared/contexts/PanesContext.tsx` with the 3-tier allocator while keeping the existing visibility gate in place for this phase. Tier 1 still returns the ideal heights unchanged, Tier 2 preserves generations height until the editor hits `MIN_EDITOR_HEIGHT`, and Tier 3 uses the derived denominator formula with an inline comment explaining the test-contract derivation.

Reran the full `PanesContext` suite with the repo vitest config after the tiered allocator change. All 13 tests passed, confirming the 3 previously failing allocator cases were fixed without regressing the gate-dependent coverage.

Removed the visibility short-circuit from `getEffectivePaneHeights`, deleted `isEditorVisible` and `isGenerationsVisible` from the helper signature, and updated the provider call site to stop passing them. Left `editorVisible` and `generationsVisible` intact in `PanesContext.tsx` because they are still consumed by the context value.

Updated the two affected assertions in `src/shared/contexts/__tests__/PanesContext.test.tsx` so initial state and editor-only state now expect the precomputed coexistence height (`effectiveEditorHeight` `300`). Added `effective heights remain stable across visibility toggle`, which records heights with no panes open, then asserts the same values after `none -> editor -> both`.

Reran the full `PanesContext` suite after removing the gate and updating the assertions. All 14 tests passed, including the new stability test that checks identical effective heights across hidden, editor-only, and both-visible states.

Ran the downstream consumer tests for `LayoutMainContent` and `useBottomOffset` with no edits to those test files. Both suites passed, confirming the visibility-gated consumer logic still behaves correctly with the allocator always precomputing coexistence heights.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Confirmed exactly 3 allocator tests failed on the configured baseline run, with failure output showing the old 70:30 behavior (`420` instead of `300`, `420` instead of `200`, and `200` instead of `156`).
The Tier 1 ideal-fit branch in `getEffectivePaneHeights` still returns `idealEditorPaneHeight` and `generationsPaneHeight` unchanged when their sum fits within the viewport.
After the tiered allocator patch and before removing the gate, the full `PanesContext` suite passed 13/13 tests.

After deleting the visibility gate, the helper signature and provider call site no longer referenced `isEditorVisible` or `isGenerationsVisible`, and the updated `PanesContext` test suite loaded and passed successfully.
Updated both affected assertions (`effectiveEditorHeight` in the initial-state and solo-editor tests) and added a stability test that explicitly toggles `none -> editor -> both`.
The new stability test captures both effective heights before any pane is visible and asserts the same editor and generations values after editor-only and both-visible transitions.
`LayoutMainContent.test.tsx` and `useBottomOffset.test.ts` both passed under the configured vitest run without any edits to those files.
